title: lichen_l2.c: lichen_l2_recv validates iface then ignores it via ARG_UNUSED
description:
## Location
firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:274-281

## Issue
The lichen_l2_recv() function validates the iface parameter for NULL, then immediately marks it as unused:

```c
static enum net_verdict lichen_l2_recv(struct net_if *iface,
                                       struct net_pkt *pkt)
{
    /* Validate iface parameter (project-LICHEN-1www.25) */
    if (iface == NULL) {
        LOG_ERR("lichen_l2_recv: iface is NULL");
        return NET_DROP;
    }

    ARG_UNUSED(iface);
    ARG_UNUSED(pkt);
    ...
```

The NULL check suggests the code expects to use iface, but then ARG_UNUSED indicates it is not used. This is contradictory.

## What goes wrong
- Confuses future maintainers: is the parameter used or not?
- If Zephyr guarantees iface is never NULL for L2 recv callbacks, the check is dead code
- If Zephyr does NOT guarantee non-NULL, then ARG_UNUSED is misleading documentation
- Similar pattern appears in lichen_l2_enable() (lines 403-409)

## Fix
Either:
1. Remove the NULL check if Zephyr guarantees non-NULL iface, and keep ARG_UNUSED
2. Keep the NULL check and remove ARG_UNUSED, with a comment explaining why we validate but dont use
3. Add a comment explaining the NULL check is defensive against future Zephyr API changes
